bordered_location_frame.note: Lorcana "Location" card type has a distinct frame layout (wider right margin, taller footer band). Not a crop defect.

bordered_quest_promo_frame.note: Sets Q1/Q2 (a promo/quest product line) use a consistently different, more symmetric crop (left approx right approx 72-75px, top approx 88px) vs the main numbered sets. Internally self-consistent, so not flagged as an anomaly, just a distinct population.

tinted_border_foil_outliers.note: Cards whose corner pixels are dark but not pure black (black_level>20 vs normal ~11) — likely foil/holo border treatment interfering with simple thresholding. Excluded from main stats.

footer_emblem_nonbordered_fullart.note: Template matching is much noisier on full-art (Enchanted/Special/full-art Epic-Legendary-Iconic) cards because background art varies. Only high-score matches are trustworthy; low scorers listed separately.

verdict.crop_is_constant_overall: Mostly yes for the dominant character-frame bordered population; a small set of well-explained exceptions exist (Location card type frame, ~16 foil-tinted-border cards, Q1/Q2 promo-quest subset with a different fixed crop).
verdict.cost_hexagon_note: The ink-cost hexagon shape/size is NOT a reliable pixel-perfect crop anchor: its apparent top edge varies by ~18px depending on per-card content (e.g. an inkwell/splash icon rendered above the hexagon for some cards), independent of rarity or set. Template matching (raw grayscale and gradient-based) also failed to generalize across cards (typical scores 0.4-0.5, frequently <0.5) because the hexagon interior (ink color fill + cost digit glyph) varies too much card-to-card. This anchor was abandoned in favor of the footer emblem and the black-frame edges/footer-band edge, which are geometry-only (not art-content) signals and matched far more consistently.
verdict.footer_emblem_y_bordered: 1899-1900 in 98.8% of high-confidence (score>0.6) matches (n=2660/2851 bordered cards scored above threshold)
verdict.footer_emblem_y_fullart: 1899-1900 in all high-confidence (score>0.55) matches (n=22 strict Special/foil template matches + many Epic/Legendary/Iconic full-art at ~0.68 score also at y=1899)
verdict.bordered_border_left_right_top_px: left mode=60, right mode=60, top mode=66 (top is NOT the ~33px-larger value the physical-card note anticipated; see caveat)
verdict.asymmetry_caveat: The physical-card reference measurement (~1.4mm / ~33px more cropped at top than bottom, at 23.1px/mm) could not be directly cross-checked against a bottom-frame pixel measurement in this analysis, because the bottom of the card is occupied by the black rarity/footer band (by design) rather than card art, so a symmetric "black frame" bottom_px measurement analogous to left/right/top is not meaningful — bottom_px was intentionally skipped per the method. The footer black band TOP edge (1906-1907, i.e. ~141-142px up from the 2048px-tall canvas bottom) is the best available bottom-side anchor and it is extremely constant (std < 1.1px), which supports "the crop is constant" but does not by itself prove/disprove the specific 33px top/bottom asymmetry claim -- that would require knowing the true (uncropped) card bleed dimensions.
verdict.top_px_measurement_caveat: top_px is measured as the first row (median across sampled columns) where pixel intensity exceeds black_level+30, scanning the top of the card. For roughly 6% of bordered character-frame cards, dark or busy artwork sits close enough to the top frame edge that this threshold approach overshoots the true border edge by tens to a couple hundred px (verified by manual per-column inspection, e.g. card 1-123 has a dark night-sky illustration starting right at the frame edge). This is a measurement-method limitation, not evidence of real crop variance -- left_px/right_px and the footer black-band-top and footer-emblem-y anchors are far more robust (>98-99 percent within 2px of mode) and are the primary evidence for crop-is-constant. top_px mode (66) still matches those more robust anchors' implied top position on the majority (~93 percent) of cards where the simple threshold method is not confounded by dark art.

topup_2026_07_19.note: 15 set-13 high-number (Epic full-art) cards appended to anchors_percard.csv via card_db/anchor_survey.py (the original survey script was lost with a sandbox; emblem convention reverse-validated 100/100 legacy rows to 4dp - see script docstring). footer_emblem_nonbordered_fullart block recomputed from the current CSV. footer_emblem_bordered block left as originally published (its n_total=2835 predates a late legacy CSV update; current CSV has 2851 bordered rows, 2660 with score>0.6, consistent with the verdict text).
topup_2026_07_19.findings: 14/15 are standard full-art layout: footer emblem at (642,1899), std-template score 0.67-0.68 - same layout-locked crop as sets 1-12; render_crop_bias_mm assumption holds unchanged for the set-13 Epic tail. 13-222 (Hundred Acre Wood - Hunny Campsite) is a LANDSCAPE Enchanted-style Location card: emblem not at the portrait-layout anchor, flagged low-score like other special layouts.
